Dalai Lama

q: You win more from losing than winning.
h:
“You win more from losing than winning.” —
Morgan Wootten

q: To love means loving the unlovable.
h:
“To love means loving the unlovable.” —
Gilbert Chesterton

q: Do you want to know who you are? Don't ask. Act! Action will delineate and define you.
h:
“Do you want to know who you are? Don't ask. Act! Action will delineate and define you.” —
Thomas Jefferson

q: It doesn't matter where you are, you are nowhere compared to where you can go.
h:
“It doesn't matter where you are, you are nowhere compared to where you can go.” —
Bob Proctor

q: Everyone must choose one of two pains: The pain of discipline or the pain of regret.
h:
“Everyone must choose one of two pains: The pain of discipline or the pain of regret.” —
Jim Rohn

q: The less people know, the more stubbornly they know it.
h:
“The less people know, the more stubbornly they know it.” —
Osho

q: The real measure of your wealth is how much you'd be worth if you lost all your money.
h:
“The real measure of your wealth is how much you'd be worth if you lost all your money.” —
Unknown

q: Your mind will make you rich or poor, depending on the use you put to it.
h:
“Your mind will make you rich or poor, depending on the use you put to it.” —
Brian Tracy

q: Get mad, then get over it.
h:
“Get mad, then get over it.” —
Colin Powell

q: What you get by achieving your goals is not as important as what you become by achieving your goals.
h:
“What you get by achieving your goals is not as important as what you become by achieving your goals.” —
Henry David Thoreau

q: Heart is what separates the good from the great.
h:
“Heart is what separates the good from the great.” —
Michael Jordan

q: If what you're doing is not your passion, you have nothing to lose.
h:
“If what you're doing is not your passion, you have nothing to lose.” —
Celestine Chua

q: Knowing others is wisdom, knowing yourself is enlightenment.
h:
“Knowing others is wisdom, knowing yourself is enlightenment.” —
Lao Tzu

q: It's not what you pay a man, but what he costs you that counts.
h:
“It's not what you pay a man, but what he costs you that counts.” —
Will Rogers

q: It takes a great deal of bravery to stand up to our enemies, but just as much to stand up to our friends.
h:
“It takes a great deal of bravery to stand up to our enemies, but just as much to stand up to our friends.” —
Albus Dumbledore

q: Our attitude toward life determines life's attitude towards us.
h:
“Our attitude toward life determines life's attitude towards us.” —
Earl Nightingale

q: Loving thoughts and actions are clearly beneficial for our physical and mental health.
h:
“Loving thoughts and actions are clearly beneficial for our physical and mental health.” —
Dalai Lama

q: The first rule of business; treat others like they want to treat you.
h:
“The first rule of business; treat others like they want to treat you.” —
Charles Dickens

q: Happiness is a choice, not a result. Nothing will make you happy until you choose to be happy.
h:
“Happiness is a choice, not a result. Nothing will make you happy until you choose to be happy.” —
Ralph Marston

q: The man who moved the mountain was the one who began carrying away the smallest stones.
h:
“The man who moved the mountain was the one who began carrying away the smallest stones.” —
Zen Proverb

q: The size of your success is measured by the strength of your desire; the size of your dream; and how you handle disappointment along the way.
h:
“The size of your success is measured by the strength of your desire; the size of your dream; and how you handle disappointment along the way.” —
Robert Kiyosaki

q: If you want peace, you won't get it with violence.
h:
“If you want peace, you won't get it with violence.” —
John Lennon

q: If you spend too much time thinking about a thing, you'll never get it done.
h:
“If you spend too much time thinking about a thing, you'll never get it done. ” —
Bruce Lee

q: Knowledge has to be improved, challenged, and increased constantly, or it vanishes.
h:
“Knowledge has to be improved, challenged, and increased constantly, or it vanishes.” —
Peter Drucker

q: If you see a get rich quick scheme, that's someone else trying to get rich off of you.
h:
“If you see a get rich quick scheme, that's someone else trying to get rich off of you.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: It is literally true that you can succeed best and quickest by helping others succeed.
h:
“It is literally true that you can succeed best and quickest by helping others succeed.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live, remember that.
h:
“It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live, remember that.” —
Albus Dumbledore

q: A single act of kindness throws out roots in all directions, and the roots spring up and make new trees.
h:
“A single act of kindness throws out roots in all directions, and the roots spring up and make new trees.” —
Amelia Earhart

q: We will outstretch the hand if you unclench your fist.
h:
“We will outstretch the hand if you unclench your fist.” —
Barack Obama

q: People spend too much time doing and not enough time thinking about what they should be doing.
h:
“People spend too much time doing and not enough time thinking about what they should be doing.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: Peace is letting it be. Letting life flow, letting emotions flow through you.
h:
“Peace is letting it be. Letting life flow, letting emotions flow through you.” —
Kamal Ravikant

q: The road to success is always under construction.
h:
“The road to success is always under construction.” —
Steve Harvey

q: Wherever you are, it's the place you need to be.
h:
“Wherever you are, it's the place you need to be.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: By changing nothing, nothing changes.
h:
“By changing nothing, nothing changes.” —
Celestine Chua

q: Don't let your mind stop you.
h:
“Don't let your mind stop you.” —
Steve Harvey

q: A man, as a general rule, owes very little to what he is born with - a man is what he makes of himself.
h:
“A man, as a general rule, owes very little to what he is born with - a man is what he makes of himself.” —
Alexander Graham Bell

q: If you haven't the strength to impose your own terms upon life, then you must accept the terms it offers you.
h:
“If you haven't the strength to impose your own terms upon life, then you must accept the terms it offers you.” —
T.S. Eliot

q: A thing must be loved before it is lovable.
h:
“A thing must be loved before it is lovable.” —
Gilbert Chesterton

q: When we strive to become better than we are, everything around us becomes better, too.
h:
“When we strive to become better than we are, everything around us becomes better, too.” —
Paulo Coelho

q: The art of being wise is knowing what to overlook.
h:
“The art of being wise is knowing what to overlook.” —
William James

q: Happiness is not the absence of problems, it's the ability to deal with them.
h:
“Happiness is not the absence of problems, it's the ability to deal with them.” —
Steve Maraboli

q: The wise learn many things from their enemies.
h:
“The wise learn many things from their enemies.” —
Aristophanes

q: So long as we are being remembered, we remain alive.
h:
“So long as we are being remembered, we remain alive.” —
Carlos Ruiz Zafon

q: Strive for excellence, not perfection, because we don't live in a perfect world.
h:
“Strive for excellence, not perfection, because we don't live in a perfect world.” —
Joyce Meyer

q: The more we value things, the less we value ourselves.
h:
“The more we value things, the less we value ourselves. ” —
Bruce Lee

q: Turn your wounds into wisdom.
h:
“Turn your wounds into wisdom. ” —
Oprah Winfrey

q: Do what you feel in your heart to be right - for you'll be criticized anyway.
h:
“Do what you feel in your heart to be right - for you'll be criticized anyway.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: If you want peace, accept. If you want suffering, expect.
h:
“If you want peace, accept. If you want suffering, expect.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: Longevity is a fleeting moment that lasts forever.
h:
“Longevity is a fleeting moment that lasts forever.” —
Vidal Sassoon

q: It doesn't matter what you do, only how well you do it.
h:
“It doesn't matter what you do, only how well you do it.” —
Dan Millman

q: Every defeat, every heartbreak every loss, contains its own seed, its own lesson on how to improve your performance the next time.
h:
“Every defeat, every heartbreak every loss, contains its own seed, its own lesson on how to improve your performance the next time.” —
Og Mandino

q: Nothing has more strength than dire necessity.
h:
“Nothing has more strength than dire necessity.” —
Euripides

q: The moment you doubt whether you can fly, you cease for ever to be able to do it.
h:
“The moment you doubt whether you can fly, you cease for ever to be able to do it.” —
James Matthew Barrie

q: You must find the place inside yourself where nothing is impossible.
h:
“You must find the place inside yourself where nothing is impossible.” —
Les Brown

q: Don't ever be afraid to put yourself in a lesser position so that you can learn something from someone who knows more.
h:
“Don't ever be afraid to put yourself in a lesser position so that you can learn something from someone who knows more.” —
Steve Harvey

q: Happiness is the meaning and the purpose of life, the whole aim and end of human existence.
h:
“Happiness is the meaning and the purpose of life, the whole aim and end of human existence.” —
Aristotle

q: I alone cannot change the world, but I can cast a stone across the waters to create many ripples.
h:
“I alone cannot change the world, but I can cast a stone across the waters to create many ripples.” —
Mother Teresa

q: If you do not conquer self, you will be conquered by self.
h:
“If you do not conquer self, you will be conquered by self.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.
h:
“To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.” —
Ralph Waldo Emerson

q: The consequences of today are determined by the actions of the past. To change your future, alter your decisions today.
h:
“The consequences of today are determined by the actions of the past. To change your future, alter your decisions today.” —
Unknown

q: Death is as sure for that which is born, as birth is for that which is dead. Therefore grieve not for what is inevitable.
h:
“Death is as sure for that which is born, as birth is for that which is dead. Therefore grieve not for what is inevitable.” —
Bhagavad Gita

q: Successful people do what unsuccessful people are not willing to do.
h:
“Successful people do what unsuccessful people are not willing to do.” —
Jim Rohn

q: Numbing the pain for a while will make it worse when you finally feel it.
h:
“Numbing the pain for a while will make it worse when you finally feel it.” —
Albus Dumbledore

q: Success comes to those who become success conscious.
h:
“Success comes to those who become success conscious.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: Be a master of change rather than a victim of change.
h:
“Be a master of change rather than a victim of change.” —
Brian Tracy

q: Do the hard jobs first. The easy jobs will take care of themselves.
h:
“Do the hard jobs first. The easy jobs will take care of themselves.” —
Dale Carnegie

q: The journey is what brings us happiness not the destination.
h:
“The journey is what brings us happiness not the destination.” —
Dan Millman

q: The biggest room in the world is the room for improvement.
h:
“The biggest room in the world is the room for improvement.” —
Unknown

q: How you think is as important as as what you think.
h:
“How you think is as important as as what you think.” —
Unknown

q: Knowledge is a treasure, but practice is the key to it.
h:
“Knowledge is a treasure, but practice is the key to it.” —
Lao Tzu

q: You talk when you cease to be at peace with your thoughts.
h:
“You talk when you cease to be at peace with your thoughts.” —
Kahlil Gibran

q: You can't tell big dreams to small minded people.
h:
“You can't tell big dreams to small minded people.” —
Steve Harvey

q: Develop success from failures. Discouragement and failure are two of the surest stepping stones to success.
h:
“Develop success from failures. Discouragement and failure are two of the surest stepping stones to success.” —
Dale Carnegie

q: When something is important enough, you do it even if the odds are not in your favor.
h:
“When something is important enough, you do it even if the odds are not in your favor.” —
Elon Musk

q: A mountain never yields to the wind no matter how strong it is.
h:
“A mountain never yields to the wind no matter how strong it is.” —
Zen Proverb

q: The wise man reads both books and life itself.
h:
“The wise man reads both books and life itself.” —
Lin Yutang

q: No tree, it is said, can grow to heaven unless its roots reach down to hell.
h:
“No tree, it is said, can grow to heaven unless its roots reach down to hell.” —
Carl Jung

q: Duty makes us do things well, but love makes us do them beautifully.
h:
“Duty makes us do things well, but love makes us do them beautifully. ” —
Zig Ziglar

q: Forever is composed of now's.
h:
“Forever is composed of now's.” —
Emily Dickinson

q: If you want to know what God thinks about money just look at the people He gives it to.
h:
“If you want to know what God thinks about money just look at the people He gives it to.” —
Alexander Pope

q: Every day is lost in which we do not learn something useful. Man has no nobler or more valuable possession than time.
h:
“Every day is lost in which we do not learn something useful. Man has no nobler or more valuable possession than time.” —
Thomas Jefferson

q: The birth of the mind is the death of the senses.
h:
“The birth of the mind is the death of the senses.” —
Dan Millman

q: Deliberately seek the company of people who influence you to think and act on building the life you desire.
h:
“Deliberately seek the company of people who influence you to think and act on building the life you desire.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: Success is liking yourself, liking what you do, and liking how you do it.
h:
“Success is liking yourself, liking what you do, and liking how you do it.” —
Maya Angelou
